content.problemStatement:
This problem has subtasks.

Given an array BBB containing MMM (where MMM is odd) integers, you have to perform the following operation on BBB until it consists of only a single element.


Choose **distinct** indices i,i,i, j,j,j, kkk of the array BBB.
Remove Bi,Bj,BkB_i, B_j, B_kBi​,Bj​,Bk​ from the array.
Append (Bi+Bj−Bk)(B_i + B_j - B_k)(Bi​+Bj​−Bk​) to BBB.
Note that the operation reduces the length of BBB by 222.

For example, if B=[2,5,3,4,2]B = [2, 5, 3, 4, 2]B=[2,5,3,4,2], you can choose indices (3,1,4)(3, 1, 4)(3,1,4), delete the values B3,B1,B4B_3, B_1, B_4B3​,B1​,B4​ from BBB, and append B3+B1−B4=3+2−4=1B_3 + B_1 - B_4 = 3+2-4 = 1B3​+B1​−B4​=3+2−4=1 to it, obtaining B=[5,2,1]B = [5, 2, 1]B=[5,2,1].

Your aim is to maximize the value of the final element remaining in the array.

Let this maximum value be f(B)f(B)f(B).

You are given an array AAA of length NNN. Answer QQQ queries on it.

In the iii-th query, you are given an integer KiK_iKi​, which is guaranteed to be odd.

Compute f([A1,A2,…,AKi])f([A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_{K_i}])f([A1​,A2​,…,AKi​​]).

That is, compute the answer when considering only the first KiK_iKi​ elements of AAA.

Each query is independent, so the array remains unchanged across queries.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of three lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ — the length of the array and the number of queries.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
The third line contains QQQ space-separated integers K1,K2,…,KQK_1, K_2, \ldots, K_QK1​,K2​,…,KQ​.




Output Format
For each test case, output QQQ space-separated numbers: the answers for each query in order.
constraints: 1≤Ki≤N 1 \leq K_i \leq N1≤Ki​≤N for each 1≤i≤Q1 \leq i \leq Q1≤i≤Q
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Query 111: The array to consider is [3,−1,2][3, -1, 2][3,−1,2]. It's optimal to choose the triple of indices (1,3,2)(1, 3, 2)(1,3,2), which turns the array into [6][6][6] (since A1+A3−A2=3+2−(−1)=6A_1+A_3-A_2 = 3+2-(-1) = 6A1​+A3​−A2​=3+2−(−1)=6).
This is optimal.
Query 222: The array to consider is [3][3][3]. No operations can be performed, and the answer is just 333.
Query 333: Now, we consider the full array. One optimal sequence of moves is:

Choose (2,3,1)(2, 3, 1)(2,3,1), turning the array into [4,5,−2][4, 5, -2][4,5,−2].
Choose (1,2,3)(1, 2, 3)(1,2,3), turning the array into [11][11][11].
